{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "6-foot fall protection trigger height ignored — no fall protection provided where work occurs at or above 6 ft",
    "Workers exposed to uncontrolled fall hazard at elevations meeting or exceeding the OSHA trigger threshold"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately at any elevation at or above 6 ft",
    "Install compliant fall protection system (guardrails, safety nets, or personal fall arrest system) before resuming elevated work",
    "Verify anchor points meet 5,000 lb strength requirement or 2:1 safety factor per applicable standard",
    "Retrain workers on fall protection trigger heights and requirements"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart M requires fall protection at heights of 6 ft or more in residential construction. The observed condition indicates the 6-ft trigger was ignored, meaning workers are operating at elevation without required fall protection. This is a critical, non-compliant deficiency creating immediate life-safety risk.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M — 1926.501(b)(1) and (b)(2) — 6 ft trigger height for residential construction",
    "OSHA 1910.140 — personal fall protection systems requirements",
    "OSHA 1926.502 — fall protection systems criteria and practices"
  ]
}
